subtasks.description: Extract contract renewal dates and key metadata (vendor, contract ID, current status) from SAM system documents
Query SQL warehouse to retrieve consumption trends (spend, usage volume, trend direction) for the past 12 months, grouped by contract/vendor

Reconcile extracted renewal dates with consumption trends to assess renewal risk (prioritize high-spend/high-consumption contracts nearing expiry), generate risk classification, and prepare procurement notification payload with vendor contact details and risk assessment

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks are required: (1) extract SAM renewal metadata (small tier — straightforward structured field extraction from documents); (2) query consumption trends from SQL warehouse (mid tier — aggregation across 12-month history with grouping); (3) cross_recon to reconcile renewal dates with consumption, classify risk, and prepare the notification (mid tier — combines two data sources, applies business logic for prioritization, and generates the alert payload). No separate policy_action is needed because risk assessment and alerting are operational decisions within cross_recon, not governance policy evaluation. No multitool_plan is needed because the workflow is linear and does not require orchestrating multiple external tools from the registry.

output.sql: SELECT cf.publisher AS vendor_name, cf.license_id AS contract_id, DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date) AS month, SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS total_spend, SUM(cf.daily_active_seats) AS total_usage_volume, COUNT(cf.event_id) AS event_count, SUM(cf.amount_usd) - LAG(SUM(cf.amount_usd)) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.license_id ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)) AS spend_change_vs_prev_month, CASE WHEN SUM(cf.amount_usd) > LAG(SUM(cf.amount_usd)) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.license_id ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)) THEN 'INCREASING' WHEN SUM(cf.amount_usd) < LAG(SUM(cf.amount_usd)) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.license_id ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)) THEN 'DECREASING' WHEN LAG(SUM(cf.amount_usd)) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.license_id ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)) IS NULL THEN 'BASELINE' ELSE 'STABLE' END AS trend_direction, SUM(SUM(cf.amount_usd)) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.license_id ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)) AS cumulative_spend_12mo, AVG(SUM(cf.daily_active_seats)) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.license_id ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date) ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS rolling_3mo_avg_seats FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE cf.event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '12 months' AND cf.event_date < CURRENT_DATE GROUP BY cf.publisher, cf.license_id, DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date) ORDER BY cf.publisher, cf.license_id, month

procurement_notification_payload.message: Contract renewal risk assessment could not be completed due to null upstream data from SAM system extract. MongoDB source returned no contract metadata and SQL consumption query produced no matching records. Immediate investigation required to restore data pipeline integrity before renewal risk can be evaluated.

action_items.action: Verify SAM system connectivity and re-run extract_renewal_dates upstream task
Confirm consumption_facts table has records for the relevant publisher and license_id within the last 12 months
Re-trigger reconciliation workflow once upstream data is restored
Escalate to procurement manager if data cannot be retrieved within 24 hours
